title: Pericallis tussilaginis
intro: Pericallis tussilaginis, also known as Flax-leaved Chirita or Pericallis, is a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities that may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Pericallis tussilaginis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as an infusion or decoction, and is also used in the form of a tincture or a syrup to treat respiratory issues and other ailments.
taxonomy: Pericallis tussilaginis is classified within the family Asteraceae. This classification places it in the order Asterales. The species is further categorized as a member of the genus Pericallis. Within the Asteraceae family, Pericallis tussilaginis is part of the subfamily Carduoideae. It is native to the Canary Islands and the western coast of Africa.
